title: Substrate v2 Cross-Cutting Hook
description: Declarative manifest for a substrate v2 cross-cutting hook. Hooks fire at lifecycle events (workflow-start, workflow-step-completion, workflow-completion, session-start, session-end, file-change) regardless of which workflow ran. They are matched against the firing context via the `matches` filter and executed by the orchestrator.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Only `v2.0` is recognized in this release.

id.description: Unique hook id within the workspace.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of what this hook does and when it fires.

trigger.description: Lifecycle event(s) at which this hook fires. ALL listed triggers will invoke the hook.

matches.description: Filter for which firing contexts invoke this hook. ALL fields must match.

workflow-id.description: Match only firings of this exact workflow id.

workflow-kind.description: Match only firings whose workflow.kind equals this value.

exit-code.description: Match only firings whose exit code equals this value, or `any` for unconditional. Only meaningful for `workflow-completion`.

step-id.description: Match only firings of this exact step id. Only meaningful for `workflow-step-completion`.

enabled.description: Whether the hook is active. Defaults to true.

order.description: Execution priority when multiple hooks match. Lower numbers run first. Defaults to 100.

step.description: The action to perform when the hook fires.

type.description: Step type. `run-deterministic` shells out to a deterministic command; `noop` is a placeholder for hooks whose handler is implemented in-process.

command.description: Shell command to invoke for `run-deterministic` steps.

pass-result.description: Pass the firing context's exit code / result to the command via stdin (JSON).

fail-on-error.description: If true, a non-zero exit from the hook fails the workflow. Defaults to false (hooks are advisory).

handler.description: Substrate-internal handler name for `noop` steps. Used by built-in hooks whose logic lives in TS (e.g. `auto-drift-detect` skeleton for B3).
